2026-04-18T09:17:44.0337152Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0338082Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0338845Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0339619Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0340413Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0340818Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0341087Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-18T09:17:44.0341687Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0342091Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0342430Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0342934Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-18T09:17:44.0343387Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-18T09:20:35.2605472Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2605664Z   Key changes from the commit:
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2606521Z   1. **Observability**: Added `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` (structured logging for all incoming HTTP requests), diagnostic logging in endpoint handlers, startup configuration logging
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2607344Z   2. **API surface**: 
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2607784Z      - Renamed `ServerUserAgent*` → `ServerVersion*` (types, methods, properties)
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2608522Z      - `AddAgentServerUserAgent()` → `AddAgentServerVersion()` + new `AddAgentServerLogging()`/`UseAgentServerLogging()` 
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2609407Z      - Internalized telemetry types and added `x-agent-session-id` response header on all protocol endpoints with SHA-256-based session ID derivation
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2610259Z   3. **Developer experience**: startup configuration logging, log isolation key presence, mask storage URLs
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2610723Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2611810Z   Now I'm checking what's actually documented in the READMEs to see if these changes are properly surfaced—the Core README mentions the new logging methods in troubleshooting, but I need to verify coverage for the session ID header and other telemetry changes.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2613471Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2614812Z   Looking at the Responses README, it documents the OpenTelemetry traces but is missing details on the new session ID behavior, deterministic derivation, and startup configuration logging.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2615992Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2617388Z   The Invocations README has a good property table and mentions session IDs, but doesn't cover the response header on all endpoints, the new startup logger, isolation key logging, or that `InvocationsActivitySource` is now internal.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2619021Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2620711Z   The biggest gaps across all documentation are the startup configuration logging feature, the session ID derivation algorithm, and which classes became internal—these are breaking changes that need clear documentation. isolation key presence logging (security-relevant - values are never logged)
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2622807Z

2026-04-18T09:20:35.2628501Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. Looking at the READMEs vs the CHANGELOGs, I can identify documentation gaps:
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2629140Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2629363Z   **Responses README gaps:**
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2630334Z   1. **Missing `x-agent-session-id` response header documentation** - The CHANGELOG says it's added on ALL protocol endpoints, but the README doesn't mention this at all. Users building clients against this server need to know about this header.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2632023Z   2. **Missing startup configuration logging documentation** - The CHANGELOG mentions `ResponsesStartupLogger` which logs storage provider type, model, etc. on startup. The README doesn't mention this diagnostic feature.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2633989Z   3. **Missing mention that `ResponsesActivitySource` and `ResponsesTracingConstants` are now internal** - The CHANGELOG marks these as breaking changes. The README troubleshooting section still says "emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source" without noting it's internal.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2635340Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2635513Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2636127Z   4. **Missing documentation for isolation key enforcement** - Chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints is a new feature, but not documented in the README.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2637230Z   5. **Missing session ID derivation documentation** - Deterministic SHA-256-based session ID is mentioned in the Responses CHANGELOG but not in the README.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2637879Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2638052Z   **Core README gaps:**
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2638927Z   1. **Startup configuration logging** - The CHANGELOG says platform environment, connectivity, host options, and registered protocols are logged at `Information` level. The README doesn't document what gets logged at startup.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2640112Z   2. **Breaking change: `AgentHostTelemetry` is internal** - mentioned in changelog but README still references it.
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2640629Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2640895Z   I'm noticing a pattern here with the Core README as well...
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2641232Z   
2026-04-18T09:20:35.2644627Z   the troubleshooting section just tells users to enable ASP.NET Core logging without explaining what startup logging actually contains. Looking at the bigger picture, the Responses package seems to have the most critical gaps—the x-agent-session-id header is a new protocol-level behavior clients will encounter, ResponsesActivitySource and Response... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2372 chars]

responseId, retrieved status and output count\\n- POST /responses/{id}/cancel: responseId and result status\\n- DELETE /responses/{id}: responseId and success\\n- GET /responses/{id}/input_items: responseId\\n- Orchestrator: handler type name on invocation\\n\\nThese logs complement the Core inbound middleware (HTTP-level) with\\nresponse-specific context for debugging storage deserialization the pipeline.\\n- ResponseEndpointHandler: cache GetConversationId() result to avoid\\n  repeated JSON parsing across partition key, logging, and session ID.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot endpoint response\\nMUST include x-agent-session-id as a response header.\\n\\nResponses protocol:\\n- Add SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter endpoint filter (outermost, wraps\\n  exception filter) that reads resolved session ID from HttpContext.Items\\n  and falls back to FoundryEnvironment.SessionId\\n- Store resolved session ID in HttpContext.Items from POST (after B39\\n  resolution), GET, extensions\\n\\nRename all ServerUserAgent* types, files, methods, and properties to\\nServerVersion* — the header identifies the server version stack, not a\\nuser-agent string.\\n\\nType renames:\\n- ServerUserAgentRegistry → ServerVersionRegistry\\n- ServerUserAgentMiddleware → ServerVersionMiddleware\\n- AgentHostBuilder.UserAgentRegistry → VersionRegistry\\n\\nExtension method restructure:\\n- AddAgentServerUserAgent() → AddAgentServerVersion() (version only)\\n- UseAgentServerUserAgent() → UseAgentServerVersion()\\n- NEW: AddAgentServerLogging() — registers was confusing. 2026-04-18T09:20:35.5108871Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T09:20:22.410409382Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58314 (commit 54ef6f748) by @RaviPidaparthi\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58314 added several significant features and breaking changes to the AgentServer Responses library:\n- `x-agent-session-id` response header is now returned on **all** Responses protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8\n- Deterministic SHA-256-based session ID derivation (matching Python SDK: `\"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\"` → 63-char hex)\n- `ResponsesActivitySource` and `ResponsesTracingConstants` were made **internal** (breaking change for users who referenced them for custom spans)\n- Startup configuration logging via `ResponsesStartupLogger` (storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, event stream TTL)\n- Chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints\n- Isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) on all endpoint logs (values are never logged)\n- Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The README does not document the `x-agent-session-id` response header that clients will now receive on every protocol endpoint — users building client code against this server need to know to expect and optionally consume this header\n- The README troubleshooting/logging section still implies `ResponsesActivitySource` is publicly usable (\"emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source\") but does not note it is now internal — any handler author who was referencing this type directly will hit a compile error with no guidance\n- The deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256 of `\"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\"`) is not documented in the README; this is important for operators who need to understand how session affinity works across requests\n- The startup configuration logging (what the server logs at startup, and which fields are masked for security) is absent from the README troubleshooting section\n- Chat isolation key enforcement behavior (mismatched key returns 404 indistinguishable from not-found) is not documented in the README Key Concepts or Troubleshooting sections\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\n\n**1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` response header to Key Concepts**\n\nAfter the \"### Thread safety\" subsection, add a new subsection:\n\n```markdown\n### Session ID Response Header\n\nEvery Responses protocol endpoint — POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — includes an\n`x-agent-session-id` response header per the agent server specification §8. The value is the\nresolved session ID for the request, derived in the following priority order:\n\n1. `agent_session_id` from the POST request body\n2. `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable\n3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of `\"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\"` (63 lowercase hex chars), where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id`\n4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available\n\nClients that need to maintain session affinity across a multi-turn conversation can read this header from the POST response and supply it as `agent_session_id` in subsequent requests.\n```\n\n**1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section**\n\nReplace:\n```markdown\n### Logging\n\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\n```\n\nWith:\n```markdown\n### Logging\n\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry distributed traces using the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source name. The `ResponsesActivitySource` class is internal to the framework; do not reference it directly in handler code.\n\n**Startup logging:** When the host starts, `ResponsesStartupLogger` logs the active configuration at `Information` level:\n- Storage provider type (e.g., `FoundryStorageProvider` or `InMemoryResponsesProvider`)\n- Default model\n- D
